tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: You are given a CT image. What organ is shown in the image?

dialogs.thought: First, identify modality/anatomy on the axial CT slice to orient which abdominal organ predominates.

content.content: Axial contrast-enhanced CT image of the upper abdomen. A large homogeneous solid organ occupies most of the left side of the image (patient right), with a bright contrast-filled stomach/bowel loop on the right side of the image (patient left). Two arrows point to a region within the large solid organ.

dialogs.thought: Check for any embedded labels or annotations that explicitly name the organ.

arguments.attribute: Identify the organ and describe its appearance/location on this axial CT slice

dialogs.thought: Confirm the large solid organ’s imaging characteristics and typical location to determine the organ shown.

content.content: The boxed region shows a large wedge-shaped solid parenchymal organ in the right upper abdomen with relatively homogeneous enhancement, consistent with liver parenchyma.

dialogs.content: The image shows the liver, which is a large, vital organ in the human body.
